# Dr. Kacey Kiesman — Naturopathic Doctor, Kelowna BC

**Last updated:** 2026-05-21

> Dr. Kacey Kiesman is a licensed Naturopathic Doctor (ND) and Registered Nurse (RN) practicing in Kelowna, British Columbia, Canada. She provides evidence-informed naturopathic medicine, IV therapy, and physician-led aesthetic treatments at Springbok Medical Clinic. No referral is required.

---

## About Dr. Kacey Kiesman

Dr. Kacey Kiesman is a Naturopathic Doctor and Registered Nurse based in Kelowna, BC. She practices evidence-informed naturopathic medicine and aesthetic care, with a focus on hormonal health, digestion, women's health, fatigue, IV therapy, and aesthetic treatments. She holds dual credentials as a Naturopathic Doctor (ND) and Registered Nurse (RN), and grew up in Prince Rupert, BC before relocating to Kelowna.

She practices at Springbok Medical Clinic in central Kelowna and accepts patients without a referral. Initial naturopathic consultations are 60 minutes and may be covered by extended health benefits in BC.

---

## What is a Naturopathic Doctor?

A Naturopathic Doctor (ND) is a licensed primary healthcare practitioner trained in both conventional medical sciences and natural therapies. In British Columbia, NDs complete a four-year doctoral program after pre-medical undergraduate studies and must pass board examinations regulated by the College of Naturopathic Physicians of BC (CNPBC). NDs in BC can order lab work, perform physical exams, and prescribe many medications.

Naturopathic medicine is a regulated health profession that combines evidence-based natural therapies with modern diagnostics, prescription medications when appropriate, and a strong focus on prevention. Care is centered on identifying and addressing the root causes of illness or imbalance through individualized treatment plans that may include nutrition, supplements, lifestyle support, diagnostic testing, and prescriptions when needed. The goal is to support the whole person and promote long-term, sustainable health.

**Naturopathic doctor vs. medical doctor (MD):** Both are licensed to diagnose and treat. MDs typically focus on pharmaceutical and surgical management; NDs focus on root-cause investigation and combine evidence-based natural therapies (nutrition, botanical medicine, IV therapy, lifestyle counselling) with prescription medications and conventional care.

**Are naturopathic visits covered by MSP in British Columbia?** No. MSP (Medical Services Plan) does not cover naturopathic visits in BC. However, most extended health insurance plans in BC reimburse naturopathic appointments. Direct billing is available at this clinic for most major insurers.

**Do I need a referral to see a naturopathic doctor in Kelowna?** No referral is required. Patients can book directly online or by calling (778) 940-9811.

---

## Naturopathic Services

Dr. Kiesman offers evidence-informed naturopathic care across these areas:

- **Women's Health** — Hormonal balance, PCOS, endometriosis, perimenopause and menopause, PMS, acne, menstrual irregularities, thyroid health, fertility support
- **Digestive Health** — IBS, SIBO, bloating, food sensitivities, leaky gut, constipation, gut microbiome optimization
- **Fatigue & Burnout** — Chronic fatigue, adrenal dysregulation, burnout, low energy, sleep optimization
- **Weight Optimization** — Metabolic and hormonal contributors to weight, sustainable nutrition and lifestyle strategies
- **Preventative Care** — Comprehensive lab work, personalized nutrition, longevity-focused lifestyle medicine

### Naturopathic Pricing (CAD)

| Service | Duration | Price |
|---|---|---|
| Initial Consultation | 60 min | $280 |
| Follow-Up Visit | 15 min | $85 |
| Follow-Up Visit | 20 min | $105 |
| Follow-Up Visit | 30 min | $140 |
| Follow-Up Visit | 45 min | $205 |
| Free Discovery Visit | 15 min | Complimentary |
| Prescription Fill | — | $30 |

### Naturopathic Visit Details

- **Initial Consultation** — A full 60-minute session covering your complete health history, current concerns, lifestyle, and goals. Labs and/or testing may be recommended to gain further insight into your current health picture. An initial care plan is built together before you leave.
- **Follow-Up Visits** — Return visits range from 15 to 45 minutes depending on what needs to be addressed. If you're unsure which length to book, the 30-minute follow-up is a good default for most check-ins.
- **Lab Testing** — Functional lab testing is often recommended, including hormonal panels, thyroid function, nutrient levels, GI stool testing, and more.
- **Insurance Coverage** — Many extended health plans in BC cover naturopathic visits. Direct billing is available to most insurance companies. Patients are encouraged to contact their insurer prior to their first visit to confirm coverage.
- **Prescription Refill** — Existing patients can request a prescription refill for a $30 CAD fee, no follow-up appointment required.
- **Before Your Visit** — After booking, patients receive intake forms by email. Please email any recent lab results prior to your appointment.

### Visit Formats

- **In Person** — At Springbok Medical Clinic, 590 McKay Ave #200, Kelowna, BC
- **Video / Telemedicine** — Secure telemedicine appointments available from anywhere in BC
- **Phone** — Convenient telephone follow-up appointments available from anywhere in BC

---

## IV Therapy in Kelowna

IV therapy delivers vitamins, minerals, and amino acids directly into the bloodstream for higher absorption than oral supplementation. Each IV is mixed and administered by Dr. Kiesman based on assessment of patient needs. Direct billing to most insurance companies is available for select IV services.

**Is an IV consultation required first?** Yes. A 20-minute IV Eligibility Visit ($70) is required for new patients before a first IV treatment, to confirm clinical appropriateness, review medications, and screen for contraindications.

**Is IV therapy covered by MSP?** No. IV therapy is private pay. Some extended health plans partially reimburse naturopathic IV services — confirm with your insurer.

### IV Therapy Pricing (CAD)

| Service | Details | Price |
|---|---|---|
| IV Eligibility Visit (new patients) | 20 min | $70 |
| IV Vitamin Therapy | 100 ml · 45 min | $140 |
| IV Vitamin Therapy | 250 ml · 60 min | $185 |
| Glutathione IV | 45 min | $150 |
| Glutathione Add-on | Add-on to other IVs | $50–$70 |
| Iron IV Consult | — | $70 |
| Iron IV Administration | — | $150 |

---

## IM Injections in Kelowna

Intramuscular (IM) vitamin injections deliver targeted vitamins, minerals, and amino acids directly into the muscle for efficient absorption and rapid availability. Commonly used to support energy, immunity, metabolism, stress resilience, and overall wellness.

**Is an eligibility visit required first?** Yes. A 20-minute IM Eligibility Visit ($70) is required for new patients before a first injection.

### IM Injection Pricing (CAD)

| Service | Details | Price |
|---|---|---|
| IM Eligibility Visit (new patients) | 20 min | $70 |
| B12 Injection | 15 min | $40 |
| B-Complex Injection | 15 min | $40 |
| B12 / B-Complex Injection | 15 min | $55 |
| Vitamin D3 Injection (bloodwork required) | 15 min | $65 |
| CoQ10 Injection | 15 min | $55 |
| MIC Injection | 15 min | $50 |
| L-Carnitine Injection | 15 min | $50 |
| MIC / L-Carnitine Injection | 15 min | $65 |
| NAD+ Injection (25 mg) | 15 min | $55 |
| NAD+ Injection (50 mg) | 15 min | $85 |

---

## Iron IV Therapy

Iron IV therapy delivers iron directly into the bloodstream to restore iron stores in patients with iron deficiency or iron-deficiency anemia, often when oral iron is poorly tolerated or insufficient. A pre-iron consultation is required to review bloodwork (ferritin, hemoglobin) and confirm suitability.

| Service | Price |
|---|---|
| Pre-Iron Consultation | $70 |
| Iron IV Administration | $150 |

---

## Aesthetic Treatments

All aesthetic treatments are physician-led and private pay (not covered by MSP or insurance).

### Botox / Neuromodulators

Neuromodulators (such as Botox) temporarily relax targeted facial muscles to soften fine lines and wrinkles, including forehead lines, frown lines (glabellar 11s), crow's feet, and bunny lines. Results typically appear within 7–14 days and last 3–4 months.

**Price:** $10 per unit (Canadian dollars).

### Dermal Filler

Hyaluronic acid dermal filler restores lost volume and enhances facial contours including cheeks, lips, jawline, and tear troughs. Results are visible immediately and last 6–18 months depending on product and area.

**Price:** $600 per syringe (Canadian dollars).

### Morpheus8 BURST RF Microneedling

Morpheus8 BURST is an advanced radiofrequency (RF) microneedling treatment that combines microneedling with bipolar radiofrequency energy to remodel deeper layers of skin. It treats skin laxity, fine lines, acne scars, and contour irregularities by stimulating collagen and elastin. A typical plan is 3 sessions spaced 4–6 weeks apart, with results developing over 3 months.

**Morpheus8 BURST pricing (CAD):**

| Area | Duration | Initial | Return |
|---|---|---|---|
| Face | 105 min | $850 | $850 |
| Face & Neck | 105 min | $1,000 | $1,000 |
| Face, Neck & Chest | 105 min | $1,500 | $1,500 |
| Stretch Marks | 105 min | $900 | $900 |
| Underside of Upper Arms | 105 min | $800 | $800 |

**Morpheus8 Resurfacing pricing (CAD):**

| Area | Duration | Initial | Return |
|---|---|---|---|
| Face | 105 min | $700 | $700 |
| Face & Neck | 105 min | $900 | $900 |

### Lumecca Peak IPL

Lumecca Peak IPL is a high-intensity pulsed light treatment that targets sun damage, hyperpigmentation, age spots, redness, rosacea, and broken capillaries. It is suitable for face, neck, chest, shoulders, and hands. Typical plans involve 2–3 sessions.

**Lumecca IPL pricing (CAD):**

| Area | Initial | Return |
|---|---|---|
| Face | $350 | $350 |
| Chest | $200 | $200 |
| Shoulders | $250 | $250 |
| Face, Neck & Chest | $500 | $500 |
| Face, Neck, Chest & Shoulders | $650 | $650 |
| Hands | $150 | $150 |
| Spot Treatment | $100 | $100 |

### DiolazeXL Laser Hair Removal

DiolazeXL is a diode laser hair removal system effective for permanent hair reduction on most body areas. Treatment is faster and more comfortable than older laser systems thanks to built-in cooling. A typical plan is 6–8 sessions spaced 4–8 weeks apart.

**DiolazeXL pricing (CAD):** $60–$400 per session, depending on treatment area. See https://drkaceykiesman.com/laser.html for the full area-by-area pricing list.

---

## Treatment Packages

Multi-session aesthetic packages with no expiry are available for Morpheus8 BURST, Lumecca Peak IPL, and DiolazeXL laser hair removal. Bundle pricing offers savings compared to per-session rates.

**Example packages (CAD):**

| Package | Sessions | Price |
|---|---|---|
| Morpheus8 BURST — Face | 3 | $2,125 |
| Morpheus8 BURST — Face & Neck | 3 | $2,500 |
| Morpheus8 BURST — Face, Neck & Chest | 3 | $3,750 |
| Morpheus8 BURST — Underarms (Hyperhidrosis) | 3 | $2,200 |
| Tight & Bright (Morpheus8 BURST Face & Neck ×2 + Lumecca IPL Face, Neck & Chest ×1) | 3 | $2,000 |
| DP4 Microneedling SkinPen | 4 | $1,200 |

See https://drkaceykiesman.com/packages.html for the complete list.

---

## Acupuncture & Cupping

**Acupuncture** is a targeted treatment for musculoskeletal pain and dysfunction. It can reduce pain, improve mobility, and support recovery from injuries, tension, and chronic MSK conditions by promoting circulation and modulating overactive pain pathways.

**Cupping** therapy uses suction to target tension, improve circulation, and support recovery. It is often used alongside acupuncture to help reduce muscle tightness, promote relaxation, and encourage the body's natural healing response.

| Service | Initial | Follow-Up |
|---|---|---|
| Acupuncture | $150 | $120 |
| Cupping | $150 | $120 |

---

## FAQ

**Q: Where is Dr. Kacey Kiesman's clinic located?**
A: Springbok Medical Clinic, 590 McKay Ave #200, Kelowna, BC V1Y 5A8. The clinic offers on-site parking and is wheelchair accessible.

**Q: Do I need a referral to see a naturopath in Kelowna?**
A: No referral is required. Patients can book directly online at springbokmedicalspa.janeapp.com or by calling (778) 940-9811.

**Q: How much does a naturopathic consultation cost in Kelowna?**
A: Initial consultations are $280 CAD for 60 minutes. Follow-up visits range from $85 (15 min) to $205 (45 min). A complimentary 15-minute Discovery Visit is also available.

**Q: Does MSP cover naturopathic medicine in BC?**
A: No. MSP (Medical Services Plan) does not cover naturopathic visits in British Columbia. Most extended health insurance plans do cover naturopath visits — direct billing is available at this clinic.

**Q: How much does Botox cost in Kelowna?**
A: Botox is priced at $10 per unit (CAD) at Dr. Kacey Kiesman's clinic. The total per treatment depends on the area treated and units used.

**Q: How much does Morpheus8 cost in Kelowna?**
A: Morpheus8 BURST sessions range from $800 (upper arms) to $1,500 (face, neck & chest) per session. Resurfacing variants start at $700 for the face. 3-session packages with no expiry start at $2,125.

**Q: How many Morpheus8 sessions are needed?**
A: A typical plan is 3 sessions spaced 4–6 weeks apart, with results developing progressively over 3 months as collagen rebuilds.

**Q: Is Lumecca IPL safe for all skin types?**
A: Lumecca Peak IPL is best suited for Fitzpatrick skin types I–IV. A consultation is required to assess eligibility, especially for darker skin tones.

**Q: How long does laser hair removal take to see results?**
A: Most patients see meaningful hair reduction after 4–6 DiolazeXL sessions spaced 4–8 weeks apart. A typical full plan is 6–8 sessions.

**Q: Can I do Botox and filler in the same visit?**
A: Yes. Combining Botox and filler in one visit is common; Dr. Kiesman customizes treatment plans for natural-looking, balanced results.

**Q: Do you offer virtual naturopathic appointments?**
A: Yes. Virtual naturopathic consultations are available across British Columbia.

**Q: Does Dr. Kiesman work alongside my family doctor?**
A: Yes. Naturopathic medicine works best as a complement to conventional medical care. Dr. Kiesman is happy to communicate with your GP or specialist when appropriate and to review recent lab work or investigations you've had done.

**Q: Which naturopathic follow-up length should I book?**
A: If you're unsure, a 30-minute follow-up ($140) is a good default for most check-ins. Book the 45-minute option ($205) if you have multiple concerns or are due for a comprehensive review. The 15- or 20-minute options ($85 / $105) work well for focused questions or lab result reviews.

**Q: How long does it take to see results with naturopathic care?**
A: This depends on what you're working on. Some people notice changes within a few weeks. Those addressing hormonal or chronic digestive issues may need a few months to see meaningful progress. Realistic timelines are set at the initial visit.

**Q: What does IV therapy help with?**
A: IV therapy may help support hydration, energy levels, recovery from illness or travel, and general wellness. IV vitamin therapy is currently under-studied and is not a proven treatment.

**Q: Who should avoid IV therapy?**
A: IV therapy may not be recommended for people with kidney disease, heart failure, allergies to any of the vitamins used, or certain chronic health conditions. A provider review of medical history is done before treatment.

**Q: Can I eat before my IV?**
A: Yes. Eating a light meal and drinking water before an IV appointment is recommended to help you feel more comfortable during the infusion.

**Q: Are there side effects of IV therapy?**
A: Side effects are uncommon but may include mild bruising at the IV site, tasting the vitamins in your mouth during the drip, and temporary lightheadedness. Serious reactions are rare and patients are monitored throughout treatment. All risks are reviewed at the eligibility visit.

**Q: Is an IV consultation required before a first IV in Kelowna?**
A: Yes. A 20-minute IV Eligibility Visit ($70) is required for new patients before a first IV or IM injection. Treatment may begin the same day if appropriate.

**Q: Is iron IV therapy right for me?**
A: Iron IV therapy is appropriate for patients with confirmed iron deficiency or iron-deficiency anemia. Lab testing (ferritin, hemoglobin) is required to confirm suitability and is ordered at the pre-iron IV consult ($70) unless you have recent labs within 3 months. The Iron IV Administration is $150 (105-minute appointment).

**Q: Do I need bloodwork before an iron IV in Kelowna?**
A: Yes. A pre-iron IV consult ($70) is required before treatment. Existing bloodwork can be sent in prior to the visit; additional labs will be ordered at the consult if needed.

**Q: How long does an iron infusion take?**
A: The iron IV therapy appointment is 105 minutes. This allows time for the infusion to be administered slowly and safely, and for monitoring during and after treatment.

**Q: How soon will I feel better after an iron IV?**
A: Individual results vary depending on the severity of deficiency. Many patients may start to notice improvements in energy and fatigue within 1–2 weeks, with full iron stores typically replenishing over 6–8 weeks.

**Q: Are there side effects from an iron infusion?**
A: Most people tolerate iron infusions well. Mild side effects may include temporary nausea, headache, or discomfort at the IV site. Patients are monitored throughout the infusion. Serious reactions are rare but possible, and provider review of medical history happens before treatment.

**Q: Is iron IV therapy covered by insurance in BC?**
A: Iron IV therapy is a private service and is not covered by MSP. Some extended health plans may provide partial coverage for iron when purchased at the pharmacy, and the iron administration fee can be submitted to extended benefits under naturopathic services. Direct billing is available to most insurance companies.

**Q: What languages does the clinic offer?**
A: Services are provided in English.

**Q: How do I book an appointment?**
A: Book online at https://springbokmedicalspa.janeapp.com/ or call (778) 940-9811. Many appointment types allow same-week booking.
